booking_notes: Sparpreis Europa saver fares available. Seat reservations €5.20 optional on ICE. DB Navigator app excellent.
notes: Largest state operator in Europe. Punctuality declined in recent years. Exited night trains in 2016 (now ÖBB Nightjet operates most). Major infrastructure investment ongoing (Riedbahn, Hamburg-Berlin).

aliases: Société Nationale des Chemins de fer Français
booking_notes: TGV requires mandatory reservation. Ouigo is low-cost subsidiary. Intercités de Nuit for night trains. TER regional trains no reservation needed.
notes: French state operator. TGV mandatory reservation. Ouigo low-cost is separate brand. Intercités de Nuit serves French regions (Paris-Briançon, Paris-Latour-de-Carol). Paris-Nice and Paris-Toulouse night trains discontinued 2020 — not restored.

booking_notes: Nightjet at nightjet.com or oebb.at. Sparschiene saver tickets from €9.90. Excellent mobile app.
notes: Europe's largest night train operator (Nightjet). RailJet premium service. Excellent booking platform. WESTbahn competitor on Wien-Salzburg corridor.

booking_notes: Supersaver tickets from CHF 16 with half-fare card. Swiss Travel Pass for tourists. TGV Lyria joint with SNCF. sbb.ch is excellent.
notes: Arguably the best rail operator in Europe. Excellent punctuality (92%+). Not in EU but in Schengen. Switzerland is expensive — warn users. SBB API exists but has signup friction.

booking_notes: Frecciarossa requires reservation. Super Economy from €19.90. InterCity Notte for night trains. Frecciarossa France competes on Paris-Milano.
notes: Italy's state operator. Genuine HSR competition with Italo/NTV. ICN night trains to Sicily cross the Messina strait via train ferry. Regional tickets must be validated.

aliases: Red Nacional de los Ferrocarriles Españoles
booking_notes: Renfe website notoriously clunky — Trainline alternative. AVE Promo from €25. Ouigo España and Iryo compete on HSR corridors. Iberian broad gauge on conventional lines.
notes: Spain's HSR network is 2nd largest worldwide. Iberian broad gauge on conventional lines. NO direct Spain-Portugal train link — discontinued. Renfe website UI problems well known.

booking_notes: No reservation needed for domestic IC. Flat pricing — no advance discount. nsinternational.com for cross-border. NS API is excellent (free, real-time).
notes: One of the simplest train systems in Europe. Flat pricing — no advance booking needed. Amsterdam Centraal major international hub. NS API freely available.

booking_notes: No domestic reservation needed. Weekend Ticket 50% off. Go Pass 1 for under-26 (€6.60/trip). Brussels has 3 main stations — check ticket.
notes: Dense network for small country. Domestic travel is walk-up. Brussels is a major Eurostar and ICE hub. iRail API (irail.be) free for real-time data.

booking_notes: EIP Pendolino mandatory reservation. TLK budget option. Booking horizon only 30 days. DB Sparpreis Europa often cheaper for international routes.
notes: Massive investment ongoing. Rail Baltica under construction. Pendolino EIP excellent on Warszawa-Kraków-Katowice-Gdańsk. PKP PLK manages infrastructure separately.

booking_notes: SuperCity Pendolino reservation required. RegioJet and Leo Express compete on price. Můj vlak app recommended.
notes: Dense rail network, one of the densest in Europe. Runs EuroNight sleeper and couchette services: Metropol (Praha-Budapest), the Praha-Budapest-Munich and Wien EuroNight that splits and joins coaches overnight at Bohumin, and the Praha-Przemysl night train (dep 22:16) toward the Ukraine border. RegioJet is a major private competitor; RailJet is a joint venture with OBB on Praha-Wien.

booking_notes: IC Bratislava-Košice €16-22. Bratislava-Wien €11.50 one-way. RegioJet also runs international through Slovakia.
notes: Small network. Bratislava-Wien is one of Europe's closest capital pairs (~60 km). High Tatras Electric Railway (TEŽ) is scenic narrow-gauge.

booking_notes: Budapest has 3 stations: Keleti (East), Nyugati (West), Déli (South). Domestic IC requires €3-5 reservation. RailJet joint with ÖBB.
notes: Budapest is major Central European hub. MÁV app improved significantly. Budapest-București night train is classic route. Major investment in rolling stock renewal.

booking_notes: ICS Ljubljana-Maribor from €8. International to Austria/Germany via ÖBB. Slovenia compact — rail is straightforward.
notes: Small but strategic network. Ljubljana is a compact hub. ICS Pendolino tilting trains on Ljubljana-Maribor. Bled nearest station is Lesce-Bled.

booking_notes: Limited English website. ICN Zagreb-Split €15-25. International windows at Zagreb Glavni kolodvor.
notes: Network in poor condition but functional. Zagreb-Split scenic but slow. Dubrovnik has NO rail. ICN tilting trains are best rolling stock.

booking_notes: IR (InterRegio) no reservation needed. Very cheap — €2-€8 local. Night trains to Budapest/Wien. Station ticket offices reliable.
notes: Cheapest trains in the EU. București-Budapest/Wien night trains. București Nord is main hub. IR trains slow but absurdly cheap.

booking_notes: Website unreliable — station purchase recommended. Very cheap: Sofia-Varna sleeper from ~€18. Sofia-Istanbul night train is classic.
notes: Most underfunded railway in the EU. Trains very slow. Sofia-Plovdiv best domestic route. Sofia-Istanbul night train is classic experience.

booking_notes: Athens-Thessaloniki IC from €19. Tempi disaster 2023 impacted network. No reliable international connections. Limited domestic network.
notes: Smallest rail network in EU relative to size. Effectively rail-isolated from Europe. Tempi disaster (Feb 2023) recovery ongoing. Athens-Thessaloniki is the only meaningful long-distance route.

booking_notes: Alfa Pendular Promo from €11 advance. Iberian broad gauge. NO direct train to Spain — international services discontinued 2020.
notes: Rail-isolated from the rest of Europe. NO trains to Spain — Sud Expresso and Lusitânia discontinued 2020. Alfa Pendular excellent on Lisboa-Porto.

booking_notes: Snabbtåg X2000 200 km/h. Nattåg to Lapland famous. Competition from Snälltåget and MTRX. SJ website excellent.
notes: Swedish monopoly on most corridors. Nattåg Stockholm-Kiruna-Narvik is iconic. Genuine competition from private operators on some routes. Denmark connection via Øresund Bridge.

booking_notes: Minipris saver fares from 249 NOK (€22). Bergensbanen scenic Oslo-Bergen. Nattog night trains. Norway is expensive.
notes: Rebranded from NSB in 2019. Bergensbanen is one of the world's most scenic railways. Nordlandsbanen crosses Arctic Circle. High prices reflect Norwegian costs.

booking_notes: Orange advance tickets from 99 DKK (€13). Rejseplanen.dk national journey planner. Øresundståg to Sweden. København-Hamburg via ferry.
notes: Orange advance tickets offer big savings. København-Hamburg train boarding a ferry is a novelty. Puttgarden-Rødby tunnel under construction. Øresund Bridge to Malmö seamless.

booking_notes: Pendolino from €8.90 advance. Night trains to Lapland popular. VR has legal monopoly. All Russian connections suspended.
notes: Legal monopoly. Russian broad gauge (1,524mm) isolates from European standard gauge. Allegro and Tolstoi to Russia suspended. Night trains to Lapland excellent. Digitraffic API available.

booking_notes: Modern Stadler FLIRT trains. Walk-up pricing. Tallinn-Tartu from €8. No practical international rail. Rail Baltica under construction.
notes: Modern trains but Russian broad gauge isolates from Europe. Rail Baltica under construction (~2028+). All Russian connections suspended.

booking_notes: Modern Stadler DMUs. Very affordable — €7-11 Riga-Daugavpils. No practical international rail. Rail Baltica coming.
notes: Functional domestic trains but Russian broad gauge. Cross-border Baltic rail minimal. Rail Baltica under construction.

booking_notes: Vilnius-Klaipeda from €15. Modern Pesa/Stadler trains. Kaunas-Warszawa standard-gauge partial. Rail Baltica under construction.
notes: Decent domestic service. Rail Baltica will transform. Kaunas-Warszawa standard-gauge section partially operational.

booking_notes: ALL domestic public transport is FREE (since March 2020). Only country with free nationwide transport. Cross-border tickets from CFL/SNCF/DB.
notes: World's only country with free public transport for all. Simple, efficient, punctual. Great base for exploring the region.

booking_notes: Dublin-Cork from €19.99 advance. Enterprise to Belfast. Small network. DART Dublin coastal. SailRail tickets available for UK.
notes: Limited network — northwest and much of west coast unserved. Ireland is an island — SailRail (train+ferry) to UK. Dublin-Belfast crosses UK border no control.

booking_notes: 20+ franchised operators. Advance Singles from £30. Split-ticketing saves money. Eurostar to continent. Caledonian Sleeper to Scotland.
notes: Complex privatized system with 20+ operators. Advance Singles offer best value. HS2 under construction. UK not in EU/Schengen — passport required.

booking_notes: YHT high-speed mandatory reservation. İstanbul-Ankara YHT from ~€8. Turistik Doğu Ekspresi books months ahead. İstanbul has Halkali and Pendik stations.
notes: Not in EU — separate visa. YHT high-speed rail is excellent. Turistik Doğu Ekspresi (Ankara-Kars) is bucket-list scenic. İstanbul-Sofia night train only practical rail link to Europe.

booking_notes: IC+ from €9. Night train sleeper from €13. Kyiv-Warszawa night train operating. ALL Russia/Belarus connections suspended. Gauge change at EU border.
notes: Massive network despite active war. Trains remarkably operational. Russian broad gauge. Western Ukraine (Lviv, Uzhhorod) functioning near-normally. ALL eastern/southern routes risk war disruption. UZ is symbol of national resilience.

booking_notes: Soko Beograd-Novi Sad from €4.50. Beograd-Bar night train legendary. Station purchase normal. Beograd Centar (Prokop) is main station now.
notes: Soko high-speed (Chinese CRRC, 200 km/h) is new. Beograd-Bar railway: 254 tunnels, 435 bridges — spectacular. Not in EU. Beograd Centar replaced old Glavna station.

booking_notes: Station purchase only. Sarajevo-Mostar ~€5. International limited. Two operators (Federation and RS) — complicated.
notes: Post-war recovery. Sarajevo-Mostar through Neretva canyon is spectacular. Two separate operators for Federation and Republika Srpska. Buses usually faster.

booking_notes: Station purchase only. Very limited service in RS entity.
notes: Republika Srpska entity operator. Separate from Federation ŽFBH. Minimal service. Bosnia's rail split is a legacy of the Dayton Agreement.

booking_notes: Station purchase. Bar-Beograd night train is the star route. Very cheap domestic. Only ~250 km of rail in Montenegro.
notes: Tiny network. Bar-Beograd railway is legendary — 254 tunnels, 435 bridges through Dinaric Alps. Podgorica-Shkodër freight only.
fare_conditions: CITY STAR: 1st passenger pays the full fare, 2 to 4 others receive a 20% discount.

booking_notes: Station purchase only. Very cheap (€3-10). Buses are faster and more frequent for most routes. International links unreliable.
notes: Minimal network. Rolling stock very old. International services unreliable. Buses are primary transport. Skopje-Bitola scenic but slow.

booking_notes: Albania's rail network is effectively defunct. No practical passenger service.
notes: EFFECTIVELY DEFUNCT. Tirana is the only European capital without any rail service. Buses and furgons (shared minibuses) are the only transport. No international rail.

booking_notes: Minimal service. Pristina-Pejë may operate. Station purchase only.
notes: Very limited passenger service. No practical international rail. Pristina-Skopje freight only. Buses are primary transport.

booking_notes: Station purchase. Chișinău-București night train ~€15-35. Gauge change at Romanian border. Broad gauge.
notes: Russian broad gauge. Chișinău-București night train via Transnistria. One of Europe's poorest rail networks. Not in EU.

booking_notes: EU TRAVEL WARNING. ALL international connections to EU suspended. Do not recommend travel.
notes: SUSPENDED_EU. EU sanctions in effect. Complicit in Russian war. All EU connections suspended. Do not route any travel through Belarus.

booking_notes: EU SANCTIONS. ALL connections to EU suspended. RZD is a sanctioned entity. Do not recommend travel.
notes: SUSPENDED_EU — EU sanctions. ALL EU connections suspended. Russian invasion of Ukraine. RZD is sanctioned. Never recommend travel to Russia.

booking_notes: Station purchase. Tbilisi-Batumi ~€7-14. Modern Stadler trains on Black Sea line. BTK railway to Turkey.
notes: Not in Interrail scheme. Russian broad gauge. Tbilisi-Batumi is the best route. Tbilisi-Yerevan night train. BTK railway to Turkey.

booking_notes: Russian Railways subsidiary. Station purchase. Yerevan-Tbilisi night train. Turkey and Azerbaijan borders closed.
notes: RZD subsidiary — sanctions concerns. Geographically isolated. Turkey and Azerbaijan borders closed. Yerevan-Tbilisi night train is the only international exit.

booking_notes: Station purchase. Baku-Ganja modern KISS trains. Baku-Tbilisi night train. Armenia border closed.
notes: Not in Interrail. Baku-Tbilisi night train. Armenia border closed. Baku suburban modern Stadler KISS. BTK railway to Georgia and Turkey.

booking_notes: Low Cost from €14.90 on sale. 300 km/h AGV. Competing directly with Trenitalia Frecciarossa. NOT covered by Interrail.
notes: MSC Group acquired majority stake 2023. Formerly NTV brand (now 'Italo' primary). Competes with Trenitalia on HSR corridors. NOT accepted by Interrail.

booking_notes: SNCF low-cost subsidiary. From €10 adults + €5 children. Limited luggage. 30 min check-in before departure. Separate from TGV inOui.
notes: SNCF low-cost subsidiary. Some Ouigo Grande Vitesse trains serve international routes (France-Spain). Mandatory check-in 30 min before departure. No Interrail acceptance.

booking_notes: Trenitalia subsidiary competing with SNCF on Paris-Milano. Frecciarossa 1000 trains.
notes: Trenitalia's French operation. Competes with SNCF TGV on Paris-Milano. Interrail accepted.

booking_notes: From €18 advance. 300 km/h ETR 1000. Madrid-Barcelona, Madrid-Valencia, etc. Interrail accepted with reservation.
notes: Private Spanish HSR operator. Trenitalia/Globalvia/Air Nostrum joint venture. Interrail accepted (with reservation). Competes with Renfe AVE and Ouigo España.

booking_notes: SNCF low-cost Spanish subsidiary. From €9. Madrid-Barcelona, Madrid-Valencia. Does NOT accept Interrail.
notes: SNCF budget HSR brand in Spain. Competes aggressively on price with AVE and Iryo. Does NOT accept Interrail.

booking_notes: Praha-Brno from €7, includes free coffee/water/wifi. Excellent service. Competes with ČD. Does NOT accept Interrail.
notes: Czech private operator. Excellent service on Central European corridors. Free drinks/wifi in all classes. Does NOT accept Interrail — warn passholders.

booking_notes: Very cheap. Praha-Kraków, Praha-Bratislava. Black trains, basic but functional. Does NOT accept Interrail.
notes: Czech private operator. Budget alternative to ČD and RegioJet. Does NOT accept Interrail.

booking_notes: From €4.99. Limited routes: Berlin-Hamburg, Berlin-Köln, Hamburg-Stuttgart, Berlin-Frankfurt. Budget green trains.
notes: FlixMobility's train brand (sister to FlixBus). Budget alternative on key German corridors. Very cheap. Does NOT accept Interrail.

booking_notes: Wien-Salzburg-München every 2h. Fixed moderate prices. No advance discounts. Accepts Interrail. Clean modern trains.
notes: Austrian private operator. Competes with ÖBB on Wien-Salzburg. Interrail accepted. Fixed pricing — no advance discounts.

booking_notes: Berlin-Malmö-Stockholm night train seasonal (April-Sept + Christmas). Domestic Malmö-Stockholm night train. Book 3-6 months ahead for summer.
notes: Swedish private operator. Only direct Germany-Sweden night train (seasonal). Interrail accepted with supplement. Goes via Øresund Bridge and Rødby-Puttgarden ferry.

booking_notes: Stockholm-Göteborg competing with SJ. Modern Stadler FLIRT trains. From 195 SEK. Does NOT accept Interrail.
notes: Hong Kong MTR-owned Swedish operator. Stockholm-Göteborg corridor only. Does NOT accept Interrail.

booking_notes: New low-cost open-access operator. Launched March 2026. Amsterdam-Berlin-Praha corridor. Very new — limited schedule.
notes: Newest entrant — launched March 2026. Amsterdam-Berlin-Praha low-cost corridor. Very new — check website for current routes and schedules. Interrail accepted with mandatory reservation.

booking_notes: London-Edinburgh from £19.90. 100% electric. East Coast Main Line open-access. Competing with LNER.
notes: UK open-access operator. London-Edinburgh only. 100% electric fleet. Competing with LNER. Interrail accepted.

booking_notes: London-Hull direct. Open-access operator.
notes: UK open-access operator. London-Hull direct service.

booking_notes: London-Sunderland, London-Bradford. Open-access operator.
notes: UK open-access operator. North-east and Yorkshire routes.

booking_notes: London Paddington-Heathrow Airport 15 min. Expensive. Elizabeth Line cheaper alternative.
notes: London airport express. Expensive — Elizabeth Line is cheaper. Interrail accepted.

booking_notes: London-Paris/Bruxelles/Amsterdam. Passport control at station. From £39 one-way. Interrail passholders: €30 seat reservation, limited allocation. Absorbed Thalys Oct 2023.
notes: Absorbed Thalys in Oct 2023. Now operates ALL high-speed services through the Channel Tunnel AND former Thalys routes (Paris-Bruxelles-Amsterdam-Köln). Interrail passholders get limited seats at €30 supplement. Passport control at station.

booking_notes: ÖBB's premium train brand. 230 km/h. Wien-Salzburg-München, Wien-Budapest, Wien-Praha, Wien-Venezia. Book on oebb.at.
notes: RailJet is the ÖBB-DB joint premium brand (not a separate company). Excellent quality — restaurant car, wifi. Operates on key Central European corridors.

booking_notes: The holding company. All trains (ex-Thalys and original Eurostar) now branded Eurostar. Red trains = former Thalys.
notes: The merged holding entity. All former Thalys and Eurostar services now under one brand. 'Eurostar Red' = former Thalys services.

booking_notes: Bruxelles-Amsterdam-Berlin-Dresden-Praha night train (3x/week). Launched 2023. From €29 seat. Newest night train operator — independent startup.
notes: Independent startup. Launched 2023. Only 3x/week — check schedule. Sleeper Deluxe compartments available. Expanding to Prague, planning future southern routes.

booking_notes: Thalys brand was absorbed into Eurostar in October 2023. Now book at eurostar.com. The red trains still exist but are branded Eurostar.
notes: ABSORBED by Eurostar October 2023. Do NOT recommend 'Thalys' — always refer to 'Eurostar (formerly Thalys)'. The red Thalys trains are now branded as Eurostar.

booking_notes: NTV brand is legacy. All trains now marketed as Italo. MSC Group acquired majority stake 2023. Book at italotreno.it.
notes: NTV brand retired. MSC acquisition completed April 2023. Brand name now 'Italo'. Use 'italo' as the operator_id for live information. This entry exists solely for alias resolution.

booking_notes: HKX was acquired by FlixMobility and folded into FlixTrain.
notes: Defunct. Absorbed by FlixTrain 2018. Legacy entry for alias resolution.

booking_notes: Locomore was crowdfunded startup. Absorbed by Leo Express 2017. No longer operates.
notes: Crowdfunded German startup. Failed financially. Absorbed by Leo Express.

booking_notes: Arriva cross-border services have been largely suspended or absorbed by other operators. Check current status for specific routes.
notes: DB subsidiary. Cross-border services largely suspended or replaced by other operators.

booking_notes: NOT YET OPERATIONAL. Planned launch 2027. Monitor website for updates. Do not recommend as a current travel option.
notes: ANNOUNCED — not yet operating. Planned Berlin-Paris and Berlin-Bruxelles night trains. Target launch 2027.
Founded by Rachel Picard and Tim Jackson, funded by Antin. 12 Alstom Avelia Horizon ordered. Paris to Bordeaux, Nantes, Rennes from around 2028. Not bookable yet.
Western France high speed, 10 Talgo Avril ordered. Paris to Nantes and Bordeaux added 2026. Launch late 2027 to early 2028. Not bookable yet.
High frequency all-stops commuter HS. 20 Alstom Avelia Stream. Paris to Lille, Strasbourg, Lyon. Targeting 2028 to 2030. Not bookable yet.
Dutch cross-channel startup, Amsterdam, Brussels, Paris, London. Targeting 2028. Not bookable yet.
Cross-channel challenger to Eurostar, 12 Alstom Avelia Stream ordered, approved Temple Mills depot access. Targeting late 2030. Not bookable yet.
Largest Polish regional operator, separate state company from PKP Intercity.
Runs the Oslo to Stavanger southern lines under franchise.
Operates Trondheim and northern Norwegian lines under franchise.
Joint Hungarian and Austrian owned, western Hungary and cross-border to Austria.
Second largest Swiss operator, Bern region and the Loetschberg.
Italian private regional, also traction partner for European Sleeper on the Italian segment.
Czech private operator, working with Georgian Railway and South Caucasus Railway on tourist trains.
Formerly MTRX, acquired by Finland's VR in 2024. Open-access Stockholm to Gothenburg.
Kazakhstan Railways, Middle Corridor transit, break of gauge to Chinese standard at the border.
Astra Trans Carpatic runs night trains in the Back-on-Track database.
Caledonian Sleeper runs night trains in the Back-on-Track database.
European Sleeper runs night trains in the Back-on-Track database.
Go-Ahead Nordic runs night trains in the Back-on-Track database.
Great Western Railway runs night trains in the Back-on-Track database.
Midnight Trains runs night trains in the Back-on-Track database.
Optima Tours runs night trains in the Back-on-Track database.
RDC / Alpen-Sylt Nachtexpress runs night trains in the Back-on-Track database.
SJ Nord runs night trains in the Back-on-Track database.
Urlaubs-Express runs night trains in the Back-on-Track database.
